title: Dock-equipment animations driven by ref reads in render never react to docking cycle
detail: WheelChock, DockLeveler, DockPlate, DockSpotter, StretchWrapMachine and WarehouseWorker receive their reactive booleans from refs that are mutated inside useFrame but read during render, e.g. line 2877 `<DockLeveler position={[0, 2, -1.5]} isDeployed={shippingDockedRef.current} />`, line 2925 `isDeployed={shippingDockedRef.current}`, line 2941 `isGuiding={ shippingStateRef.current === 'backing' || ... }`. `shippingDockedRef`/`shippingDoorsOpenRef`/`shippingStateRef` are assigned inside the useFrame loop (e.g. line 2627 `shippingDockedRef.current = truckState.phase === 'docked' || ...`). Reading `.current` during render does NOT trigger a re-render when the ref mutates. The cleanest victims are WheelChock (effect deps `[isDeployed]`, line 137) and DockLeveler (effect deps `[isDeployed]`, line 2550) — their deploy/lerp animation only re-registers when the prop value actually changes, which it never does mid-cycle. TruckBay (a non-memoized React.FC) only re-renders when its parent MillScene re-renders, and MillScene's productionSpeed prop comes from App's `useState(0.8)` slider — so re-renders are user-driven and infrequent. Net effect: wheel chocks, dock levelers, dock plates and the spotter's wave do not deploy/retract in sync with trucks docking; they stay frozen at whatever value was captured at the last unrelated re-render. The internal `isGuidingRef.current = isGuiding` in DockSpotter (line 1430) proves the author intended live reactivity.
fix: Lift the truck dock/door/phase state that drives equipment into React state (or subscribe the equipment to the production store dock-status values that useFrame already writes via setTruckDocked/updateDockStatus), so prop changes re-render the equipment. Minimal version: replace the docked/doorsOpen/phase refs that feed JSX props with useState updated in useFrame (throttled to state transitions only, which the code already detects). All edits stay inside TruckBay.tsx.
_area: L1 Correctness · DEEP giants A: FactoryExterior + TruckBay

title: Shipping dock-status update nested inside `if (receivingTruckRef.current)` block
detail: The shipping truck's HolographicDisplay status update (lines 2775-2795, `const shippingCycle = adjustedTime % CYCLE_LENGTH; ... updateDockStatus('shipping', ...)`) is mis-nested: brace-depth analysis confirms it sits inside the `if (receivingTruckRef.current) {` block opened at line 2683 and closed at line 2796. So shipping dock status only updates when the receiving truck ref is non-null. In practice this is a no-op because both `<group ref={shippingTruckRef}>` and `<group ref={receivingTruckRef}>` are rendered as a pair and attach together, so the receiving ref is always present when shipping status needs updating. Still a genuine logic-locality bug: the shipping-status block is unrelated to the receiving truck and belongs at the useFrame top level.
fix: Move the shipping-status computation/updateDockStatus block (lines 2775-2795) out of the `if (receivingTruckRef.current)` block to sit at the useFrame body level alongside the receiving-status block, mirroring the symmetric structure.
_area: L1 Correctness · DEEP giants A: FactoryExterior + TruckBay

title: Inline getTruckState arrow defeats RealisticTruck registration effect, causing register/unregister churn
detail: At line 3206 `getTruckState={() => getShippingState(performance.now() / 1000)}` (and line 3580 for receiving) a new arrow function is created on every TruckBay render. RealisticTruck's registration effect lists it as a dependency: line 3658 `}, [truckId, getTruckState]);`. Since RealisticTruck is a plain React.FC (not memoized) and getTruckState identity changes each render, the effect tears down (unregisterTruckComponents + clearTimeout) and re-registers (new setTimeout(checkAndRegister, 0)) on every parent re-render. Impact is bounded because TruckBay re-renders are infrequent (user-driven productionSpeed slider / isTabVisible / graphicsQuality), but it is wasteful churn of the truck animation registry and can briefly drop the truck from the animation manager during the 0ms timeout window.
fix: Wrap getShippingState/getReceivingState (or the inline arrows passed at 3206/3580) in useCallback so identity is stable, OR remove getTruckState from the effect dependency array at line 3658 since it is only used to register a stable accessor. Verify visually that trucks still animate.
_area: L1 Correctness · DEEP giants A: FactoryExterior + TruckBay

title: Inline workAreaBounds object literal churns WarehouseWorker registration effect
detail: At line 3116 `workAreaBounds={{ minX: -8, maxX: 8, minZ: -5, maxZ: 8 }}` (and line 3536) passes a fresh object literal each render. WarehouseWorkerWithPalletJack registers the worker in an effect with `}, [workerId, isActive, workAreaBounds]);` (line 1016), so the new object identity re-runs the effect (unregisterWorker + registerWorker) on every parent re-render. Same low-but-real churn class as the getTruckState finding. Additionally `isActive={shippingDoorsOpenRef.current}` (line 3115) reads a useFrame-mutated ref in render, so the worker's active state does not reactively update (related to the dock-equipment staleness finding).
fix: Hoist the workAreaBounds literal to a module-level const or wrap in useMemo so identity is stable across renders; this removes the registration churn without behavior change.
_area: L1 Correctness · DEEP giants A: FactoryExterior + TruckBay

title: AnimatedRiverWater ignores later flowSpeed prop changes (useRef init-once)
detail: `const uniforms = useRef({ ... flowSpeed: { value: flowSpeed } });` (lines 428-434) initializes the shader uniform from the `flowSpeed` prop only once. If a caller later changes flowSpeed, `uniforms.current.flowSpeed.value` is never updated (no effect syncs it; useFrame at line 534 only updates `time`). Same latent staleness applies to StillCanalWater's analogous uniforms ref. Not a live bug today because all call sites pass static flowSpeed (e.g. line 1216 `flowSpeed={1.2}`), but it is a correctness trap if a caller ever animates flowSpeed.
fix: Add a small useEffect that syncs `uniforms.current.flowSpeed.value = flowSpeed` when the prop changes, or document that flowSpeed is mount-only. Low risk; keep behind owner sign-off since it touches shader visuals.
_area: L1 Correctness · DEEP giants A: FactoryExterior + TruckBay

title: AnimatedFrog heart key uses Date.now(), can collide on rapid pets
detail: `const id = Date.now();` (line 1321) is used as the React key/id for spawned HeartParticle elements (line 1420 `key={h.id}`). Two pet clicks within the same millisecond produce duplicate keys, triggering React key collision warnings and potentially one heart's onComplete removing the wrong entry. Purely cosmetic Easter-egg path (clicking the pond frog), so impact is trivial.
fix: Use a monotonic counter ref (`heartCounter.current++`) or `crypto.randomUUID()` for the heart id instead of Date.now().
_area: L1 Correctness · DEEP giants A: FactoryExterior + TruckBay
